name: Code Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Code Workflow. This workflow integrates 8 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, wait, code, respondToWebhook. It contains 15 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Generate A-Z Queries
nodes.notes:
This code adds 
- a blank space
- and a letter, starting from a, b, c... up to z
It processes all the 26 possible letters.

Ex :
n8n a
n8n b
n8n c
...
n8n z

nodes.name: Google Autocomplete
nodes.notes: This httpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Loop Over Items
nodes.notes:
The 26 items (one for each alphabet letter) are treated by batches of 10.
This setting + the wait time help not to be blocked by Google's API.

nodes.name: Wait 1s
nodes.notes: This wait time is necessary, otherwise Google API might block the call and make an error.

nodes.name: Code
nodes.notes: This code node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Type a Keyword and Discover What People Search on Google

This workflow scrapes Google autocomplete results by combining **your keyword** with every letter from **A to Z**.

**Example:**  
Keyword: `n8n`  
Results:  
- n8n agent  
- n8n automation  
- n8n api  
- ...and so on

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Get Keyword
nodes.notes:
You could also get this initial keyword from :
- a line in a Google Sheet
- a webhook and a form on a website
- a messaging app like Telegram or Whatsapp

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Exporting the Keywords

You can easily add a node to export the keywords in various ways:

- via a webhook
- by email
- as a file (e.g., saved to Google Drive)
- directly to a website

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Extract Keywords
nodes.notes: This code gathers all the keywords in one list.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## Adapt the Language

Autocomplete results depend on the selected language.

You can change the `&hl=en` parameter in the **Google Autocomplete** node.  
Replace the `"en"` part with the language code of your choice.

**Examples:**  
- `&hl=fr` → French  
- `&hl=es` → Spanish  
- `&hl=de` → German

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
## Type a Keyword and Discover What People Search on Google

This workflow scrapes Google autocomplete results by combining **your keyword** with every letter from **A to Z**.

**Example:**  
Keyword: `n8n`  
Results:  
- n8n agent  
- n8n automation  
- n8n api  
- ...and so on

## Exporting the Keywords

You can easily add a node to export the keywords in various ways:

- via a webhook
- by email
- as a file (e.g., saved to Google Drive)
- directly to a website

## Adapt the Language

Autocomplete results depend on the selected language.

You can change the `&hl=en` parameter in the **Google Autocomplete** node.  
Replace the `"en"` part with the language code of your choice.

**Examples:**  
- `&hl=fr` → French  
- `&hl=es` → Spanish  
- `&hl=de` → German

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Return Keywords".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "Google Autocomplete" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait 1s" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Keyword" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
